action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 26 eval failed because predict.py attempted to denormalize a CUDA tensor with numpy arrays, triggering a TypeError when PyTorch internally called __array__ / .numpy() on the CUDA tensor. We have only ~3.4 minutes left. Analysis: No time for retraining or recomputing alphas. The proven best configuration is best2.pt + TTA(n=11) + per-step naive blending, which scored 3.6336 in iteration 25. The ensemble added in iteration 26 caused the bug. Decision: Revert predict.py to single-model inference, fix the denormalization by converting mean/std to torch tensors on the same device before arithmetic, update the deprecated autocast call, run a 1-second syntax check, and immediately re-submit best2.pt.
